step_by_step_reasoning: We model the Aqua Security architecture by identifying the Aqua SaaS platform, the customer's EKS cluster, the Aqua Enforcer DaemonSet agent, and the customer applications. We establish two main workflows: one for the Security Administrator configuring policies and receiving alerts from the Aqua SaaS console, and another for the customer's end-users interacting with protected applications while the Aqua Enforcer monitors and blocks malicious behaviors in real-time.

graph.name: Aqua Security CNAPP Protection on AWS EKS
graph.notes: Aqua Security uses a daemonset-based Enforcer agent to secure workloads running on customer EKS clusters in real-time, offering deep visibility and active blocking without impacting application availability.

nodes.name: Security Administrator
nodes.notes: Configures security profiles, monitors compliance, and reviews threat reports.

nodes.name: Aqua SaaS Management Console
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Central management plane for security analysis, alerts, policy definitions, and reporting.

nodes.name: Customer Application Pods
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Core containerized applications running on customer's managed EKS worker nodes.

nodes.name: Aqua Enforcer DaemonSet
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Deployed on each EKS node to perform real-time kernel, network, and process monitoring and inline blocking.

nodes.name: Customer End User
nodes.notes: Interacts with the customer's cloud application.

edges.notes: Analyst defines and deploys security policies on the SaaS console.
SaaS console distributes policies to Enforcers running on customer EKS nodes.
Enforcer monitors process execution, syscalls, and network activities.
Enforcer streams security telemetry and audit logs back to SaaS console.
Analyst views security alerts and compliance reports in the dashboard.
End user requests access to containerized application features.
Enforcer detects and blocks anomalous/unauthorized processes inside the container dynamically while preserving application uptime.
Allowed application traffic continues to serve the customer end user.
